mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents using standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead for AI applications. Ideal for teams looking to streamline their AI agent deployment processes.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is required to interact with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64

chmod +x kagenti-cli

sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kagenti CLI binary.
> Make the CLI executable.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetches cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Lists all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the Cluster
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti application.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies autoscaling configuration.
> Applies monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti application and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti application.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step upgrades the Kagenti application to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backs up current configurations.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with the current Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Check compatibility and use 'helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version <compatible-version>'

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase resource limits in the deployment configuration and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow the current user to access the resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure the user has the correct roles assigned using 'kubectl create clusterrolebinding'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes when running 'kubectl get nodes' and the Kagenti application running in the 'kagenti' namespace.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
